---
title: "gp3tools paper-only synthetic showcase: comprehensive Gazepoint workflow demonstration"
author: "Stefanos Balaskas"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date())`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: false
    df_print: paged
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.width = 8,
  fig.height = 5.5
)

options(dplyr.summarise.inform = FALSE)

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(readr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(knitr)
  library(tibble)
})

pkg_dir <- "C:/Users/Stefanos-PC/Documents/Rstudio/gp3tools"
if (dir.exists(pkg_dir) && file.exists(file.path(pkg_dir, "DESCRIPTION")) && requireNamespace("devtools", quietly = TRUE)) {
  devtools::load_all(pkg_dir, quiet = TRUE)
} else {
  library(gp3tools)
}

base_dir <- "C:/Users/Stefanos-PC/Desktop/gp3tools_paper_showcase"
paper_export_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "synthetic_paper_exports")
paper_output_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "rendered_html", "paper_showcase_outputs")
paper_figures_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "figures")
paper_tables_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "tables")

dir.create(paper_output_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(paper_figures_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(paper_tables_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

stopifnot(dir.exists(paper_export_dir))

is_error <- function(x) inherits(x, "error")

safe_do <- function(expr) {
  tryCatch(
    eval.parent(substitute(expr)),
    error = function(e) e,
    warning = function(w) {
      # Warnings are allowed to appear in computations, but this wrapper keeps
      # optional branches from interrupting the paper render.
      invokeRestart("muffleWarning")
    }
  )
}

safe_eval <- function(expr) {
  tryCatch(
    eval.parent(substitute(expr)),
    error = function(e) e
  )
}

object_summary <- function(x, object_name = "object") {
  if (is_error(x)) {
    tibble(
      object = object_name,
      status = "not_completed",
      class = "condition",
      rows = NA_integer_,
      columns = NA_integer_,
      message = conditionMessage(x)
    )
  } else if (is.data.frame(x)) {
    tibble(
      object = object_name,
      status = "ok",
      class = paste(class(x), collapse = "/"),
      rows = nrow(x),
      columns = ncol(x),
      message = ""
    )
  } else if (is.list(x)) {
    tibble(
      object = object_name,
      status = "ok",
      class = paste(class(x), collapse = "/"),
      rows = length(x),
      columns = NA_integer_,
      message = paste(names(x), collapse = ", ")
    )
  } else {
    tibble(
      object = object_name,
      status = "ok",
      class = paste(class(x), collapse = "/"),
      rows = length(x),
      columns = NA_integer_,
      message = ""
    )
  }
}

show_table <- function(x, n = 12, digits = 3) {
  if (is_error(x)) {
    tibble(status = "not_completed", message = conditionMessage(x)) %>%
      knitr::kable()
  } else if (is.data.frame(x)) {
    x %>%
      head(n) %>%
      knitr::kable(digits = digits)
  } else if (is.list(x)) {
    tibble(component = names(x)) %>%
      knitr::kable()
  } else {
    tibble(value = as.character(x)) %>%
      knitr::kable()
  }
}

show_component <- function(x, component, n = 12, digits = 3) {
  if (is_error(x)) {
    tibble(status = "not_completed", message = conditionMessage(x)) %>%
      knitr::kable()
  } else if (is.list(x) && component %in% names(x)) {
    show_table(x[[component]], n = n, digits = digits)
  } else {
    tibble(status = "not_available", component = component) %>%
      knitr::kable()
  }
}

num_col <- function(data, col, default = NA_real_) {
  if (!col %in% names(data)) return(rep(default, nrow(data)))
  suppressWarnings(as.numeric(data[[col]]))
}

chr_col <- function(data, col, default = NA_character_) {
  if (!col %in% names(data)) return(rep(default, nrow(data)))
  as.character(data[[col]])
}

bool_col <- function(data, col, default = TRUE) {
  if (!col %in% names(data)) return(rep(default, nrow(data)))
  x <- data[[col]]
  if (is.logical(x)) return(x)
  if (is.numeric(x) || is.integer(x)) return(x == 1)
  as.character(x) %in% c("1", "TRUE", "true", "valid", "Valid")
}

first_existing_col <- function(data, candidates, fallback = NULL) {
  hit <- candidates[candidates %in% names(data)]
  if (length(hit) > 0) return(hit[1])
  fallback
}

split_half_reliability <- function(data,
                                   outcome_col,
                                   participant_col = "subject",
                                   unit_col = "trial_global",
                                   condition_col = NULL,
                                   seed = 20260617) {
  if (!all(c(outcome_col, participant_col, unit_col) %in% names(data))) {
    return(tibble(
      outcome = outcome_col,
      n_participants = NA_integer_,
      reliability = NA_real_,
      spearman_brown = NA_real_,
      status = "missing_required_columns"
    ))
  }

  set.seed(seed)
  grouping_vars <- c(participant_col, condition_col)
  grouping_vars <- grouping_vars[!is.na(grouping_vars) & nzchar(grouping_vars)]

  scored <- data %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[outcome_col]])) %>%
    distinct(across(all_of(c(grouping_vars, unit_col))), .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(grouping_vars))) %>%
    arrange(.data[[unit_col]], .by_group = TRUE) %>%
    mutate(split_half = if_else(row_number() %% 2 == 1, "half_1", "half_2")) %>%
    ungroup()

  wide <- scored %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(c(participant_col, "split_half")))) %>%
    summarise(score = mean(.data[[outcome_col]], na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = split_half, values_from = score)

  if (!all(c("half_1", "half_2") %in% names(wide)) || nrow(wide) < 3) {
    return(tibble(
      outcome = outcome_col,
      n_participants = nrow(wide),
      reliability = NA_real_,
      spearman_brown = NA_real_,
      status = "insufficient_split_data"
    ))
  }

  r <- suppressWarnings(cor(wide$half_1, wide$half_2, use = "complete.obs"))
  sb <- ifelse(is.na(r), NA_real_, (2 * r) / (1 + r))

  tibble(
    outcome = outcome_col,
    n_participants = nrow(wide),
    reliability = r,
    spearman_brown = sb,
    status = "ok"
  )
}

media_design <- tibble(
  media_id = 0:3,
  media_name = c("Control_Ad", "Early_Target_Ad", "Late_Target_Ad", "Distractor_Heavy_Ad"),
  condition = c("control", "early_target", "late_target", "distractor_heavy")
)

aoi_geometry <- tibble(
  MEDIA_ID = rep(0:3, each = 7),
  AOI = rep(c("target", "price", "brand", "claim", "warning", "distractor", "background"), times = 4),
  x_center = rep(c(0.25, 0.72, 0.50, 0.38, 0.82, 0.18, 0.50), times = 4),
  y_center = rep(c(0.35, 0.70, 0.22, 0.58, 0.30, 0.78, 0.50), times = 4),
  width = rep(c(0.18, 0.16, 0.18, 0.18, 0.16, 0.20, 0.95), times = 4),
  height = rep(c(0.18, 0.16, 0.18, 0.18, 0.16, 0.20, 0.95), times = 4)
) %>%
  mutate(
    x_min = pmax(0, x_center - width / 2),
    x_max = pmin(1, x_center + width / 2),
    y_min = pmax(0, y_center - height / 2),
    y_max = pmin(1, y_center + height / 2)
  )
```

# Abstract


`gp3tools` is an R package for importing, validating, analysing, visualising, and reporting Gazepoint GP3 and Gazepoint Analysis eye-tracking exports. Gazepoint export folders typically contain separate all-gaze, fixation, and summary CSV files, and researchers must verify file pairing, sampling rate, tracking quality, AOI coding, pupil availability, fixation structure, preprocessing decisions, and reporting completeness before statistical modelling. `gp3tools` addresses this workflow gap by providing a reproducible bridge from proprietary Gazepoint exports to analysis-ready R objects, diagnostic summaries, model-ready tables, plots, reports, and interoperability formats for related eye-tracking packages.

This paper-only case study demonstrates the package using a larger synthetic-realistic Gazepoint export dataset created outside the package repository. The dataset contains 48 participants, four media/condition files per participant, seven AOI states, 115,200 all-gaze rows, 5,691 fixation rows, realistic missing pupil intervals, invalid and out-of-range gaze samples, participant-level quality variation, fixation files, and a summary export. The purpose of the dataset is not to test a substantive psychological or marketing hypothesis. Instead, it is used to exercise the package’s import, validation, quality-control, pupil, AOI, fixation, transition, sensitivity, model-checking, reporting, and interoperability workflows under richer conditions than a lightweight GitHub demonstration dataset.

The workflow shows that `gp3tools` can identify complete all-gaze/fixation file pairs, estimate sampling rate, flag review-required recordings, create analysis-ready all-gaze and fixation tables, audit master-table structure, perform gaze recalibration when known target coordinates are available, summarise AOI viewing behaviour, audit AOI geometry and denominators, preprocess pupil data, run interpolation and baseline-quality checks, prepare confirmatory pupil-window and AOI-window model data, fit representative LMM, GLMM, and GAMM-style models, construct AOI transition and sequence objects, run selected sensitivity checks, create exclusion recommendations, generate reporting-readiness objects, and prepare data for external workflows. The case study positions `gp3tools` as a practical workflow framework for transparent Gazepoint analysis in R. The numerical outputs are interpreted as software-demonstration outputs rather than substantive empirical findings.

**Keywords:** eye tracking; Gazepoint GP3; Gazepoint Analysis; R package; AOI analysis; pupil preprocessing; fixation analysis; GLMM; LMM; GAMM; PCHIP interpolation; reproducible research; data quality

# Introduction

Gazepoint GP3 eye trackers and Gazepoint Analysis exports are used in experimental, applied, and teaching contexts because they provide accessible eye-tracking data in CSV format. However, the exported file structure creates several practical analysis challenges. A typical project may contain one all-gaze file and one fixation file per participant, one or more Gazepoint Analysis summary exports, multiple media stimuli, AOI labels, pupil columns, gaze-validity indicators, fixation variables, and timing fields. Before modelling can begin, researchers must confirm that all files are paired correctly, that sampling rates are plausible, that gaze and pupil validity are adequate, that AOI labels are interpretable, that fixation summaries are usable, and that preprocessing decisions are documented.

These steps are often handled manually or through ad hoc scripts. Manual workflows can obscure how the analysis table was obtained, which trials were reviewed, which pupil samples were interpolated, which AOI denominators were used, and whether model results are sensitive to preprocessing choices. This is especially important for eye-tracking research because small changes in validity thresholds, AOI definitions, baseline windows, interpolation rules, or exclusion criteria can alter downstream statistical results.

`gp3tools` was developed to make this workflow explicit and reproducible for Gazepoint GP3 and Gazepoint Analysis data. The package is not intended to replace experimental-design software, calibration procedures, or substantive statistical judgement. Instead, it standardises the data-management and diagnostic steps that commonly precede confirmatory analysis. It provides tools for import, file-pair validation, sampling diagnostics, tracking-quality summaries, master-table creation, AOI summaries, pupil preprocessing, fixation summaries, transition and sequence preparation, mixed-model-ready tables, model diagnostics, exclusion recommendations, reporting checklists, and interoperability with related R eye-tracking workflows.

This case study has four aims. First, it demonstrates an end-to-end Gazepoint workflow using a synthetic-realistic export folder that can be shared without exposing private participant data. Second, it documents how `gp3tools` converts raw export folders into structured diagnostic and analysis-ready objects. Third, it shows how the package supports both confirmatory workflows, such as predefined pupil-window LMMs and AOI-window GLMMs, and exploratory or sensitivity workflows, such as GAMM trajectories, transition matrices, leave-one-unit checks, and preprocessing multiverse summaries. Fourth, it provides manuscript-facing outputs that can help researchers report quality-control, preprocessing, modelling, and readiness decisions transparently.

# Software contribution and workflow architecture

The contribution of `gp3tools` is a workflow framework for Gazepoint exports rather than a single new statistical model. The package is organised around four layers.

The first layer handles file discovery and import. It reads Gazepoint all-gaze, fixation, and summary exports; classifies export files; checks whether all-gaze and fixation files are paired; and creates harmonised sample-level objects.

The second layer handles diagnostics and quality control. It estimates sampling rate, summarises gaze and pupil validity, flags recordings requiring review, audits master-table structure, checks AOI geometry and AOI coding, examines condition-level quality imbalance, supports offline gaze recalibration when known target coordinates are available, and produces diagnostic plots and reports.

The third layer prepares analysis-ready summaries. It creates AOI-window summaries, pupil-window summaries, fixation-trial summaries, fixation-aligned data, AOI-entry objects, AOI transition tables, transition matrices, Markov-style objects, HMM-ready data, semi-Markov-ready data, pupil GAMM data, AOI GAMM data, growth-curve-style data, and model-ready tables for confirmatory mixed models.

The fourth layer supports reporting and robustness. It provides model diagnostics, nested-model comparison, model-prediction plots, leave-one-unit sensitivity, bootstrapped divergence-point estimation, preprocessing multiverse outputs, exclusion recommendations, real-data readiness gates, reporting checklists, analysis-decision audits, manuscript-ready model-table exports, and adapters for related R eye-tracking workflows.

# Demonstration dataset and paper-only scope

The demonstration dataset used in this R Markdown document is synthetic and paper-only. It was generated outside the package repository to avoid increasing package size and to keep the public GitHub package lightweight. The dataset mimics a realistic Gazepoint export folder with 48 participants, four media files per participant, four synthetic conditions, seven AOI states, gaze-coordinate noise, participant-level drift, invalid gaze samples, missing pupil intervals, fixation files, and a summary export.

The synthetic dataset is designed to exercise the software workflow. It should not be treated as empirical evidence for psychological, perceptual, or marketing claims. For example, condition differences in pupil traces, target-looking trajectories, AOI viewed time, or transition probabilities are useful because they demonstrate that the package can produce the relevant objects and figures. They should not be interpreted as substantive effects. The appropriate software-paper claim is narrower and stronger: `gp3tools` can process a realistic Gazepoint-like export folder, generate diagnostic objects, prepare analysis-ready tables, run representative models and sensitivity checks, and produce reporting-readiness outputs in a reproducible R workflow.

# 1. Paper-only synthetic export folder

The paper-only export folder is intentionally separate from the package repository. The package keeps a smaller GitHub-friendly demonstration dataset, while this paper workflow uses a richer synthetic export folder to produce more informative figures, diagnostics, and model examples.

```{r file-inventory}
export_files <- list.files(paper_export_dir)

file_inventory <- tibble(file = export_files) %>%
  mutate(
    file_type = case_when(
      grepl("_all_gaze\\.csv$", file) ~ "all_gaze",
      grepl("_fixations\\.csv$", file) ~ "fixations",
      grepl("^Data_Summary", file) ~ "summary",
      TRUE ~ "other"
    )
  )

file_inventory %>%
  count(file_type, name = "n_files") %>%
  kable()
```

```{r generator-status}
tibble(
  object = c("paper_export_dir", "generator_script"),
  path = c(
    paper_export_dir,
    file.path(base_dir, "01_create_paper_synthetic_gazepoint_exports.R")
  ),
  exists = file.exists(path)
) %>%
  kable()
```

# 2. End-to-end workflow execution

The high-level workflow is run first. This produces file-pair checks, sampling-rate diagnostics, tracking-quality diagnostics, AOI summaries, plots, CSV outputs, and an HTML diagnostic report.

```{r workflow-execution}
workflow_results <- run_gazepoint_workflow(
  export_dir = paper_export_dir,
  output_dir = paper_output_dir,
  prefix = "paper_synthetic",
  overwrite = TRUE,
  save_plots = TRUE,
  create_report = TRUE,
  report_title = "Paper-only synthetic Gazepoint showcase diagnostic report"
)

workflow_summary <- summarise_gazepoint_workflow(workflow_results)

workflow_summary %>%
  kable()
```

The workflow summary should show 48 complete file pairs, 115,200 all-gaze rows, 5,691 fixation rows, 192 sampling/quality rows, and 1,344 AOI rows if the current paper-only generator was used.

```{r workflow-output-files}
tibble(file = list.files(paper_output_dir, full.names = FALSE)) %>%
  kable()
```

# 3. File-pair and sampling diagnostics

```{r file-pair-summary}
workflow_results$file_pairs %>%
  count(status, name = "n_file_pairs") %>%
  kable()

workflow_results$file_pairs %>%
  head(12) %>%
  kable()
```

```{r sampling-diagnostics}
workflow_results$sampling %>%
  summarise(
    n_rows = n(),
    mean_hz = mean(estimated_hz, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_hz = min(estimated_hz, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_hz = max(estimated_hz, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_interval_ms = mean(mean_interval_ms, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_interval_ms = median(median_interval_ms, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  kable(digits = 3)

workflow_results$sampling %>%
  select(USER_FILE, MEDIA_ID, n_samples, duration_sec, mean_interval_ms, estimated_hz) %>%
  head(16) %>%
  kable(digits = 3)
```

```{r sampling-plot, fig.cap="Sampling-rate diagnostic plot produced by the high-level gp3tools workflow."}

sampling_plot_data <- workflow_results$sampling %>%
  mutate(
    condition = media_design$condition[match(MEDIA_ID, media_design$media_id)],
    condition = factor(condition, levels = media_design$condition),
    outside_tolerance_clean = if ("outside_tolerance" %in% names(.)) {
      tidyr::replace_na(outside_tolerance, FALSE)
    } else {
      abs(estimated_hz - 60) > 5
    }
  )

sampling_rate_clean_plot <- sampling_plot_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = condition, y = estimated_hz)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 60, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(55, 65), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.45, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(
    aes(shape = outside_tolerance_clean),
    width = 0.12,
    height = 0,
    alpha = 0.65,
    size = 1.8
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(54, 66)) +
  labs(
    x = "Condition",
    y = "Estimated sampling rate (Hz)",
    shape = "Outside tolerance",
    caption = "Solid line = nominal 60 Hz; dashed lines = ±5 Hz tolerance."
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(paper_figures_dir, "figure_sampling_rate_clean.png"),
  plot = sampling_rate_clean_plot,
  width = 7.5,
  height = 4.8,
  dpi = 300
)

sampling_rate_clean_plot
```



# 4. Tracking-quality diagnostics

The paper-only generator deliberately includes invalid gaze samples, missing pupil intervals, participant-level quality variation, and some out-of-range gaze coordinates. These features make the quality-control workflow more informative than the lightweight package demo.

```{r quality-diagnostics}
workflow_results$flagged_quality %>%
  mutate(review_required_clean = tidyr::replace_na(review_required, FALSE)) %>%
  count(review_required_clean, name = "n_rows") %>%
  kable()

workflow_results$flagged_quality %>%
  mutate(review_required_clean = tidyr::replace_na(review_required, FALSE)) %>%
  group_by(MEDIA_ID, review_required_clean) %>%
  summarise(n_rows = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  kable()
```

```{r quality-table}
workflow_results$flagged_quality %>%
  select(
    USER_FILE, MEDIA_ID, BPOGV_valid_pct, min_pupil_valid_pct_observed,
    estimated_hz, flag_low_gaze_validity, flag_low_pupil_validity,
    flag_sampling_rate, flag_short_duration, review_required
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(tidyr::replace_na(review_required, FALSE)), USER_FILE, MEDIA_ID) %>%
  head(20) %>%
  kable(digits = 2)
```

```{r quality-plot, fig.cap="Tracking-quality diagnostic plot produced by the high-level gp3tools workflow."}

quality_plot_base <- workflow_results$flagged_quality %>%
  mutate(
    condition = media_design$condition[match(MEDIA_ID, media_design$media_id)],
    condition = factor(condition, levels = media_design$condition),
    review_required_clean = tidyr::replace_na(review_required, FALSE)
  )

quality_plot_long <- quality_plot_base %>%
  transmute(
    USER_FILE,
    MEDIA_ID,
    condition,
    review_required_clean,
    metric = "Gaze-valid samples",
    percent = BPOGV_valid_pct
  )

if ("min_pupil_valid_pct_observed" %in% names(quality_plot_base) &&
    any(is.finite(quality_plot_base$min_pupil_valid_pct_observed))) {
  quality_plot_long <- bind_rows(
    quality_plot_long,
    quality_plot_base %>%
      transmute(
        USER_FILE,
        MEDIA_ID,
        condition,
        review_required_clean,
        metric = "Pupil-valid samples",
        percent = min_pupil_valid_pct_observed
      )
  )
}

tracking_quality_clean_plot <- quality_plot_long %>%
  filter(is.finite(percent)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = condition, y = percent)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 70, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.45, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(
    aes(shape = review_required_clean),
    width = 0.12,
    alpha = 0.65,
    size = 1.8
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ metric) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(50, 100)) +
  labs(
    x = "Condition",
    y = "Validity (%)",
    shape = "Review required",
    caption = "Dashed line = 70% illustrative review threshold."
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(paper_figures_dir, "figure_tracking_quality_clean.png"),
  plot = tracking_quality_clean_plot,
  width = 7.5,
  height = 4.8,
  dpi = 300
)

tracking_quality_clean_plot
```


# 5. Analysis-ready all-gaze and fixation tables

```{r analysis-data}
raw_all <- workflow_results$all_gaze
raw_fix <- workflow_results$all_fix

time_column <- grep("^TIME$|^TIME_", names(raw_all), value = TRUE)[1]
if (is.na(time_column)) time_column <- "TIME"

analysis_data <- raw_all %>%
  mutate(
    subject = gsub("_all_gaze\\.csv$", "", chr_col(raw_all, "USER_FILE")),
    media_id = num_col(raw_all, "MEDIA_ID"),
    media_name = chr_col(raw_all, "MEDIA_NAME"),
    condition = media_design$condition[match(media_id, media_design$media_id)],
    trial_global = paste(subject, media_id, sep = "_media_"),
    time = num_col(raw_all, time_column) * 1000,
    x = num_col(raw_all, "BPOGX"),
    y = num_col(raw_all, "BPOGY"),
    pupil_left = num_col(raw_all, "LPMM"),
    pupil_right = num_col(raw_all, "RPMM"),
    pupil = rowMeans(cbind(pupil_left, pupil_right), na.rm = TRUE),
    pupil = if_else(is.finite(pupil), pupil, NA_real_),
    valid = bool_col(raw_all, "BPOGV"),
    aoi_current = if_else(is.na(chr_col(raw_all, "AOI")) | chr_col(raw_all, "AOI") == "", "non_aoi", chr_col(raw_all, "AOI")),
    is_fixation = bool_col(raw_all, "FPOGV"),
    is_saccade = num_col(raw_all, "SACCADE_MAG", 0) > 0.15,
    stimulus_onset_time = 0,
    target_onset_time = case_when(
      condition == "early_target" ~ 2000,
      condition == "late_target" ~ 7000,
      condition == "distractor_heavy" ~ 4500,
      TRUE ~ 3500
    ),
    response_time = 10000,
    target_x = 0.25,
    target_y = 0.35,
    is_check_target = time <= 750 & aoi_current == "target"
  )

analysis_data %>%
  select(subject, media_id, condition, trial_global, time, x, y, pupil, valid, aoi_current) %>%
  head(12) %>%
  kable(digits = 3)
```

```{r fixation-data}
fix_time_column <- grep("^FPOGS$|^TIME$|^TIME_", names(raw_fix), value = TRUE)[1]
if (is.na(fix_time_column)) fix_time_column <- "TIME"

fixation_data <- raw_fix %>%
  mutate(
    subject = gsub("_fixations\\.csv$", "", chr_col(raw_fix, "USER_FILE")),
    media_id = num_col(raw_fix, "MEDIA_ID"),
    media_name = chr_col(raw_fix, "MEDIA_NAME"),
    condition = media_design$condition[match(media_id, media_design$media_id)],
    trial_global = paste(subject, media_id, sep = "_media_"),
    time = num_col(raw_fix, fix_time_column) * 1000,
    duration = num_col(raw_fix, "FPOGD") * 1000,
    x = num_col(raw_fix, first_existing_col(raw_fix, c("FPOGX", "BPOGX"), "BPOGX")),
    y = num_col(raw_fix, first_existing_col(raw_fix, c("FPOGY", "BPOGY"), "BPOGY")),
    valid = bool_col(raw_fix, "FPOGV"),
    aoi_current = if_else(is.na(chr_col(raw_fix, "AOI")) | chr_col(raw_fix, "AOI") == "", "non_aoi", chr_col(raw_fix, "AOI"))
  )

fixation_data %>%
  count(condition, name = "n_fixations") %>%
  kable()
```

# 6. Master-table validation and audit

```{r master-audit}
master_table <- safe_eval(create_gazepoint_master(
  gaze_data = workflow_results$all_gaze,
  time_unit = "seconds",
  user_col = "USER_FILE",
  media_col = "MEDIA_ID",
  media_name_col = "MEDIA_NAME",
  tracker_sampling_rate = 60
))

master_validation <- safe_eval(validate_gazepoint_master(
  analysis_data,
  require_pupil = TRUE,
  require_aoi = TRUE,
  fail_on_error = FALSE
))

audit_ready_master <- analysis_data %>%
  mutate(
    valid_sample = valid,
    missing_gaze = is.na(x) | is.na(y),
    missing_pupil = is.na(pupil),
    gaze_offscreen = x < 0 | x > 1 | y < 0 | y > 1,
    aoi_count = as.integer(!is.na(aoi_current) & aoi_current != "non_aoi")
  )

master_audit <- safe_eval(audit_gazepoint_master(audit_ready_master))

bind_rows(
  object_summary(master_table, "create_gazepoint_master"),
  object_summary(master_validation, "validate_gazepoint_master"),
  object_summary(master_audit, "audit_gazepoint_master")
) %>%
  kable()
```

# 7. Drift correction and gaze-signal quality

```{r drift-correction}
gaze_signal_quality <- safe_eval(audit_gazepoint_gaze_signal_quality(
  analysis_data,
  subject_col = "subject",
  condition_col = "condition",
  group_cols = c("subject", "media_id", "trial_global"),
  x_col = "x",
  y_col = "y",
  validity_cols = "valid",
  pupil_col = "pupil"
))

recalibrated_gaze <- safe_eval(recalibrate_gazepoint_gaze(
  analysis_data,
  x_col = "x",
  y_col = "y",
  target_x_col = "target_x",
  target_y_col = "target_y",
  time_col = "time",
  grouping_cols = c("subject", "media_id"),
  calibration_col = "is_check_target",
  calibration_value = TRUE,
  method = "median_shift",
  min_valid_points = 3L,
  max_shift = 0.30
))

bind_rows(
  object_summary(gaze_signal_quality, "audit_gazepoint_gaze_signal_quality"),
  object_summary(recalibrated_gaze, "recalibrate_gazepoint_gaze")
) %>%
  kable()
```

```{r drift-plot, fig.cap="Before/after gaze error for target-check rows in the paper-only synthetic dataset."}
if (!is_error(recalibrated_gaze) && is.data.frame(recalibrated_gaze) &&
    all(c("gaze_error_before_recalibration", "gaze_error_after_recalibration") %in% names(recalibrated_gaze))) {
  recalibrated_gaze %>%
    filter(is_check_target) %>%
    select(subject, media_id, condition,
           gaze_error_before_recalibration,
           gaze_error_after_recalibration) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols = c(gaze_error_before_recalibration, gaze_error_after_recalibration),
      names_to = "stage",
      values_to = "target_error"
    ) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = stage, y = target_error)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    facet_wrap(~ condition) +
    labs(x = "Calibration stage", y = "Distance from known target")
}
```

# 8. AOI summaries, geometry, coding, and verification

```{r aoi-summary}
workflow_results$aoi_table %>%
  mutate(condition = media_design$condition[match(MEDIA_ID, media_design$media_id)]) %>%
  group_by(condition, AOI) %>%
  summarise(
    n_rows = n(),
    mean_sample_time_viewed_sec = mean(sample_time_viewed_sec, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_sample_ttff_sec = mean(sample_ttff_sec, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_fixation_count = mean(fixation_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_fixation_duration_ms = mean(fixation_duration_mean_ms, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(condition, desc(mean_sample_time_viewed_sec)) %>%
  kable(digits = 3)
```

```{r aoi-viewed-plot, fig.cap="Mean AOI viewed time by condition and AOI."}
workflow_results$aoi_table %>%
  mutate(condition = media_design$condition[match(MEDIA_ID, media_design$media_id)]) %>%
  group_by(condition, AOI) %>%
  summarise(mean_viewed = mean(sample_time_viewed_sec, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = AOI, y = mean_viewed)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~ condition) +
  labs(x = "AOI", y = "Mean sample time viewed (seconds)") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

```{r aoi-audits}
geometry_audit <- safe_eval(audit_gazepoint_aoi_geometry(
  data = aoi_geometry,
  aoi_col = "AOI",
  stimulus_col = "MEDIA_ID",
  x_min_col = "x_min",
  y_min_col = "y_min",
  x_max_col = "x_max",
  y_max_col = "y_max"
))

overlap_audit <- safe_eval(audit_gazepoint_aoi_overlap(
  data = aoi_geometry,
  aoi_col = "AOI",
  stimulus_col = "MEDIA_ID",
  x_min_col = "x_min",
  y_min_col = "y_min",
  x_max_col = "x_max",
  y_max_col = "y_max"
))

coding_audit <- safe_eval(audit_gazepoint_aoi_coding_matrix(
  gaze_data = analysis_data,
  aoi_geometry = aoi_geometry,
  observed_aoi_col = "aoi_current",
  gaze_x_col = "x",
  gaze_y_col = "y",
  gaze_stimulus_col = "media_id",
  sample_id_cols = c("subject", "trial_global", "time"),
  geometry_aoi_col = "AOI",
  geometry_stimulus_col = "MEDIA_ID",
  x_min_col = "x_min",
  y_min_col = "y_min",
  x_max_col = "x_max",
  y_max_col = "y_max"
))

margin_audit <- safe_eval(audit_gazepoint_aoi_margin_sensitivity(
  gaze_data = analysis_data,
  aoi_geometry = aoi_geometry,
  gaze_x_col = "x",
  gaze_y_col = "y",
  gaze_stimulus_col = "media_id",
  sample_id_cols = c("subject", "trial_global", "time"),
  geometry_aoi_col = "AOI",
  geometry_stimulus_col = "MEDIA_ID",
  x_min_col = "x_min",
  y_min_col = "y_min",
  x_max_col = "x_max",
  y_max_col = "y_max",
  margins = c(-0.02, 0, 0.02)
))

bind_rows(
  object_summary(geometry_audit, "audit_gazepoint_aoi_geometry"),
  object_summary(overlap_audit, "audit_gazepoint_aoi_overlap"),
  object_summary(coding_audit, "audit_gazepoint_aoi_coding_matrix"),
  object_summary(margin_audit, "audit_gazepoint_aoi_margin_sensitivity")
) %>%
  kable()
```

```{r aoi-verification-plot, fig.cap="AOI geometry verification using sampled gaze points."}
aoi_verification_plot <- safe_eval(plot_gazepoint_aoi_verification(
  aoi_geometry = aoi_geometry,
  gaze_data = analysis_data %>% sample_n(min(3000, nrow(.))),
  geometry_aoi_col = "AOI",
  geometry_stimulus_col = "MEDIA_ID",
  x_min_col = "x_min",
  y_min_col = "y_min",
  x_max_col = "x_max",
  y_max_col = "y_max",
  gaze_x_col = "x",
  gaze_y_col = "y",
  gaze_stimulus_col = "media_id"
))

if (!is_error(aoi_verification_plot)) print(aoi_verification_plot)
```

# 9. Pupil preprocessing, PCHIP sensitivity, and confirmatory pupil-window models

```{r pupil-preprocessing}
pupil_flagged <- safe_eval(flag_gazepoint_pupil(
  analysis_data,
  pupil_col = "pupil",
  time_col = "time",
  group_cols = c("subject", "media_id"),
  min_pupil = 1,
  max_pupil = 8
))

pupil_artifacts <- safe_eval(flag_gazepoint_pupil_artifacts(
  analysis_data,
  pupil_col = "pupil",
  left_pupil_col = "pupil_left",
  right_pupil_col = "pupil_right",
  time_col = "time",
  missing_pupil_col = NULL,
  group_cols = c("subject", "media_id"),
  pupil_min_mm = 1,
  pupil_max_mm = 8
))

pupil_hampel <- safe_eval(flag_gazepoint_pupil_hampel(
  analysis_data,
  pupil_col = "pupil",
  time_col = "time",
  grouping_cols = c("subject", "media_id"),
  corrected_col = "pupil_hampel_corrected"
))

pupil_linear <- safe_eval(interpolate_gazepoint_pupil(
  analysis_data,
  pupil_col = "pupil",
  time_col = "time",
  group_cols = c("subject", "media_id"),
  max_gap_ms = 150
))

pupil_pchip <- safe_eval(interpolate_gazepoint_pupil_pchip(
  analysis_data,
  pupil_col = "pupil",
  time_col = "time",
  grouping_cols = c("subject", "media_id"),
  max_gap_ms = 150
))

pupil_smoothed <- safe_eval(smooth_gazepoint_pupil(
  analysis_data,
  pupil_col = "pupil",
  time_col = "time",
  group_cols = c("subject", "media_id"),
  window_samples = 5
))

bind_rows(
  object_summary(pupil_flagged, "flag_gazepoint_pupil"),
  object_summary(pupil_artifacts, "flag_gazepoint_pupil_artifacts"),
  object_summary(pupil_hampel, "flag_gazepoint_pupil_hampel"),
  object_summary(pupil_linear, "interpolate_gazepoint_pupil"),
  object_summary(pupil_pchip, "interpolate_gazepoint_pupil_pchip"),
  object_summary(pupil_smoothed, "smooth_gazepoint_pupil")
) %>%
  kable()
```

```{r pupil-timecourse, fig.cap="Pupil time course by condition in the paper-only synthetic dataset."}
pupil_timecourse_plot <- safe_eval(plot_gazepoint_pupil_timecourse(
  analysis_data,
  pupil_col = "pupil",
  time_col = "time",
  condition_col = "condition",
  bin_width_ms = 250
))

if (!is_error(pupil_timecourse_plot)) print(pupil_timecourse_plot)
```

```{r pupil-windows}
pupil_windows_raw <- safe_eval(summarise_gazepoint_pupil_windows(
  analysis_data,
  pupil_col = "pupil",
  time_col = "time",
  windows = c(0, 1000, 3000, 6000, 10000),
  group_cols = c("subject", "media_id", "condition", "trial_global"),
  min_valid_samples = 5
))

linear_data <- if (!is_error(pupil_linear) && is.data.frame(pupil_linear)) pupil_linear else analysis_data
pchip_data <- if (!is_error(pupil_pchip) && is.data.frame(pupil_pchip)) pupil_pchip else analysis_data

linear_pupil_col <- first_existing_col(
  linear_data,
  c("pupil_interpolated", "pupil_linear_interpolated", "pupil_clean", "pupil"),
  fallback = "pupil"
)

pchip_pupil_col <- first_existing_col(
  pchip_data,
  c("pupil_pchip", "pupil_pchip_interpolated", "pupil_interpolated", "pupil"),
  fallback = "pupil"
)

pupil_windows_linear <- safe_eval(summarise_gazepoint_pupil_windows(
  linear_data,
  pupil_col = linear_pupil_col,
  time_col = "time",
  windows = c(0, 1000, 3000, 6000, 10000),
  group_cols = c("subject", "media_id", "condition", "trial_global"),
  min_valid_samples = 5
))

pupil_windows_pchip <- safe_eval(summarise_gazepoint_pupil_windows(
  pchip_data,
  pupil_col = pchip_pupil_col,
  time_col = "time",
  windows = c(0, 1000, 3000, 6000, 10000),
  group_cols = c("subject", "media_id", "condition", "trial_global"),
  min_valid_samples = 5
))

bind_rows(
  object_summary(pupil_windows_raw, "raw_pupil_windows"),
  object_summary(pupil_windows_linear, "linear_interpolation_pupil_windows"),
  object_summary(pupil_windows_pchip, "pchip_interpolation_pupil_windows")
) %>%
  kable()
```

```{r pupil-interpolation-sensitivity}
compare_pupil_windows <- function(raw, linear, pchip) {
  if (is_error(raw) || is_error(linear) || is_error(pchip)) {
    return(tibble(status = "one_or_more_window_objects_not_completed"))
  }
  key_cols <- intersect(
    c("subject", "media_id", "condition", "trial_global", "window_label"),
    names(raw)
  )
  raw %>%
    select(all_of(key_cols), raw_mean_pupil = mean_pupil) %>%
    left_join(
      linear %>% select(all_of(key_cols), linear_mean_pupil = mean_pupil),
      by = key_cols
    ) %>%
    left_join(
      pchip %>% select(all_of(key_cols), pchip_mean_pupil = mean_pupil),
      by = key_cols
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      linear_minus_raw = linear_mean_pupil - raw_mean_pupil,
      pchip_minus_raw = pchip_mean_pupil - raw_mean_pupil,
      pchip_minus_linear = pchip_mean_pupil - linear_mean_pupil
    )
}

pupil_interpolation_sensitivity <- compare_pupil_windows(
  pupil_windows_raw,
  pupil_windows_linear,
  pupil_windows_pchip
)

pupil_interpolation_sensitivity %>%
  group_by(window_label) %>%
  summarise(
    n_rows = n(),
    mean_linear_minus_raw = mean(linear_minus_raw, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_pchip_minus_raw = mean(pchip_minus_raw, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_pchip_minus_linear = mean(pchip_minus_linear, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_abs_pchip_minus_raw = max(abs(pchip_minus_raw), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  kable(digits = 5)
```

```{r pupil-interpolation-plot, fig.cap="Sensitivity of pupil-window means to linear and PCHIP interpolation branches."}
if (is.data.frame(pupil_interpolation_sensitivity) &&
    all(c("linear_minus_raw", "pchip_minus_raw") %in% names(pupil_interpolation_sensitivity))) {
  pupil_interpolation_sensitivity %>%
    select(condition, window_label, linear_minus_raw, pchip_minus_raw) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols = c(linear_minus_raw, pchip_minus_raw),
      names_to = "branch",
      values_to = "difference_from_raw"
    ) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = window_label, y = difference_from_raw)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    facet_grid(branch ~ condition) +
    labs(x = "Pupil window", y = "Difference from raw pupil-window mean") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
}
```

```{r pupil-baseline-and-audits}
pupil_baseline_corrected <- safe_eval(baseline_correct_gazepoint_pupil(
  analysis_data,
  pupil_col = "pupil",
  time_col = "time",
  baseline_window = c(0, 500),
  group_cols = c("subject", "media_id"),
  baseline_method = "mean",
  min_baseline_samples = 3
))

pupil_baseline_audit_data <- if (!is_error(pupil_baseline_corrected) && is.data.frame(pupil_baseline_corrected)) {
  pupil_baseline_corrected %>%
    mutate(pupil_was_interpolated = if ("pupil_was_interpolated" %in% names(.)) pupil_was_interpolated else FALSE)
} else {
  pupil_baseline_corrected
}

pupil_baseline_audit <- if (!is_error(pupil_baseline_audit_data) && is.data.frame(pupil_baseline_audit_data)) {
  safe_eval(audit_gazepoint_pupil_baseline(
    pupil_baseline_audit_data,
    group_cols = c("subject", "media_id"),
    time_col = "time",
    pupil_col = "pupil"
  ))
} else {
  pupil_baseline_audit_data
}

pupil_gap_audit <- if (!is_error(pupil_linear) && is.data.frame(pupil_linear)) {
  safe_eval(audit_gazepoint_pupil_gaps(pupil_linear, group_cols = c("subject", "media_id")))
} else pupil_linear

pupil_imbalance_audit <- if (!is_error(pupil_linear) && is.data.frame(pupil_linear)) {
  safe_eval(audit_gazepoint_pupil_imbalance(
    pupil_linear,
    group_cols = "condition",
    pupil_col = linear_pupil_col
  ))
} else pupil_linear

pupil_overlap_audit <- safe_eval(audit_gazepoint_pupil_overlap_risk(
  analysis_data,
  group_cols = "subject",
  trial_col = "trial_global",
  time_col = "time",
  event_time_cols = c("stimulus_onset_time", "target_onset_time", "response_time"),
  window_start_ms = 0,
  window_end_ms = 2000
))

pupil_drift_audit <- safe_eval(audit_gazepoint_pupil_drift(
  analysis_data %>% mutate(trial = as.integer(factor(trial_global)), excluded_trial = FALSE),
  group_cols = "subject",
  pupil_col = "pupil",
  time_col = "time",
  order_col = "trial",
  condition_col = "condition",
  exclude_col = "excluded_trial"
))

bind_rows(
  object_summary(pupil_baseline_corrected, "baseline_correct_gazepoint_pupil"),
  object_summary(pupil_baseline_audit, "audit_gazepoint_pupil_baseline"),
  object_summary(pupil_gap_audit, "audit_gazepoint_pupil_gaps"),
  object_summary(pupil_imbalance_audit, "audit_gazepoint_pupil_imbalance"),
  object_summary(pupil_overlap_audit, "audit_gazepoint_pupil_overlap_risk"),
  object_summary(pupil_drift_audit, "audit_gazepoint_pupil_drift")
) %>%
  kable()
```

```{r pupil-models}
pupil_model_data <- if (!is_error(pupil_windows_raw)) {
  safe_eval(prepare_gazepoint_pupil_window_model_data(
    pupil_windows_raw,
    outcome_col = "mean_pupil",
    subject_col = "subject",
    condition_col = "condition",
    window_col = "window_label",
    trial_col = "trial_global",
    media_col = "media_id",
    min_valid_samples = 5,
    min_valid_prop = 0.5
  ))
} else pupil_windows_raw

pupil_lmm <- if (!is_error(pupil_model_data)) safe_eval(fit_gazepoint_pupil_window_lmm(pupil_model_data)) else pupil_model_data
pupil_sensitivity <- if (!is_error(pupil_model_data)) safe_eval(fit_gazepoint_pupil_window_sensitivity(pupil_model_data)) else pupil_model_data

pupil_gamm_data <- safe_eval(prepare_gazepoint_pupil_gamm_data(
  analysis_data,
  pupil_col = "pupil",
  time_col = "time",
  subject_col = "subject",
  condition_col = "condition",
  x_col = "x",
  y_col = "y",
  bin_width_ms = 250,
  time_window = c(0, 10000),
  min_valid_samples = 3
))

pupil_gamm <- if (!is_error(pupil_gamm_data)) {
  safe_eval(fit_gazepoint_pupil_gamm(
    pupil_gamm_data,
    pupil_col = "mean_pupil",
    time_col = "time_bin_center_ms",
    subject_col = "subject",
    condition_col = "condition",
    n_time_basis = 8
  ))
} else pupil_gamm_data

pupil_pfe_gamm <- if (!is_error(pupil_gamm_data)) {
  safe_eval(fit_gazepoint_pupil_pfe_gamm(
    pupil_gamm_data,
    pupil_col = "mean_pupil",
    time_col = "time_bin_center_ms",
    subject_col = "subject",
    condition_col = "condition",
    x_col = "mean_x",
    y_col = "mean_y",
    n_time_basis = 8,
    n_position_basis = 6
  ))
} else pupil_gamm_data

pupil_trial_features <- safe_eval(summarise_gazepoint_pupil_trial_features(
  analysis_data,
  group_cols = c("subject", "trial_global", "condition"),
  pupil_col = "pupil",
  time_col = "time",
  min_valid_samples = 5
))

candidate_pupil_outcomes <- if (!is_error(pupil_trial_features)) {
  intersect(c("mean_pupil", "early_mean_pupil", "middle_mean_pupil", "late_mean_pupil"), names(pupil_trial_features))
} else character()

pupil_reliability <- if (!is_error(pupil_trial_features) && length(candidate_pupil_outcomes) > 0) {
  safe_eval(audit_gazepoint_pupil_reliability(
    pupil_trial_features,
    outcome_cols = candidate_pupil_outcomes,
    participant_col = "subject",
    trial_col = "trial_global"
  ))
} else {
  safe_eval(stop("No suitable pupil reliability outcome columns were found."))
}

bind_rows(
  object_summary(pupil_model_data, "prepare_gazepoint_pupil_window_model_data"),
  object_summary(pupil_lmm, "fit_gazepoint_pupil_window_lmm"),
  object_summary(pupil_sensitivity, "fit_gazepoint_pupil_window_sensitivity"),
  object_summary(pupil_gamm_data, "prepare_gazepoint_pupil_gamm_data"),
  object_summary(pupil_gamm, "fit_gazepoint_pupil_gamm"),
  object_summary(pupil_pfe_gamm, "fit_gazepoint_pupil_pfe_gamm"),
  object_summary(pupil_trial_features, "summarise_gazepoint_pupil_trial_features"),
  object_summary(pupil_reliability, "audit_gazepoint_pupil_reliability")
) %>%
  kable()
```

# 10. AOI-window modelling, denominator audit, and reliability

```{r aoi-window-models}
aoi_windows_def <- tibble(
  window_label = c("early", "middle", "late"),
  window_start_ms = c(0, 1000, 3000),
  window_end_ms = c(1000, 3000, 10000)
)

aoi_windows <- safe_eval(summarise_gazepoint_aoi_windows(
  analysis_data,
  windows = aoi_windows_def,
  time_col = "time",
  aoi_col = "aoi_current",
  subject_col = "subject",
  condition_col = "condition",
  group_cols = c("subject", "media_id", "trial_global"),
  target_aoi_values = "target",
  non_aoi_values = c("non_aoi", "")
))

aoi_denominator_audit <- if (!is_error(aoi_windows) && is.data.frame(aoi_windows)) {
  safe_eval(audit_gazepoint_aoi_window_denominators(
    aoi_windows,
    window_col = "window_label",
    window_start_col = "window_start_ms",
    window_end_col = "window_end_ms",
    denominator_col = "n_valid_denominator_samples",
    total_col = "n_window_samples",
    target_col = "n_target_samples",
    condition_col = "condition",
    group_cols = c("subject", "trial_global"),
    min_denominator_samples = 5
  ))
} else aoi_windows

aoi_glmm_data <- if (!is_error(aoi_windows)) {
  safe_eval(prepare_gazepoint_aoi_glmm_data(
    aoi_windows,
    success_col = "n_target_samples",
    denominator = "valid",
    subject_col = "subject",
    condition_col = "condition",
    window_col = "window_label",
    min_denominator_samples = 5
  ))
} else aoi_windows

aoi_empirical_logit <- if (!is_error(aoi_glmm_data)) {
  safe_eval(transform_gazepoint_aoi_empirical_logit(
    aoi_glmm_data,
    numerator_col = "aoi_glmm_success",
    denominator_col = "aoi_glmm_denominator"
  ))
} else aoi_glmm_data

aoi_glmm <- if (!is_error(aoi_glmm_data)) safe_eval(fit_gazepoint_aoi_window_glmm(aoi_glmm_data)) else aoi_glmm_data
aoi_model_sensitivity <- if (!is_error(aoi_glmm_data)) safe_eval(fit_gazepoint_aoi_model_sensitivity(aoi_glmm_data)) else aoi_glmm_data

aoi_window_reliability <- if (!is_error(aoi_windows) && is.data.frame(aoi_windows)) {
  split_half_reliability(
    aoi_windows,
    outcome_col = "target_sample_prop_valid",
    participant_col = "subject",
    unit_col = "trial_global",
    condition_col = NULL
  )
} else tibble(status = "aoi_windows_unavailable")

bind_rows(
  object_summary(aoi_windows, "summarise_gazepoint_aoi_windows"),
  object_summary(aoi_denominator_audit, "audit_gazepoint_aoi_window_denominators"),
  object_summary(aoi_glmm_data, "prepare_gazepoint_aoi_glmm_data"),
  object_summary(aoi_empirical_logit, "transform_gazepoint_aoi_empirical_logit"),
  object_summary(aoi_glmm, "fit_gazepoint_aoi_window_glmm"),
  object_summary(aoi_model_sensitivity, "fit_gazepoint_aoi_model_sensitivity"),
  object_summary(aoi_window_reliability, "derived_aoi_split_half_reliability")
) %>%
  kable()

aoi_window_reliability %>% kable(digits = 3)
```

```{r aoi-gamm}
aoi_gamm_data <- safe_eval(prepare_gazepoint_aoi_gamm_data(
  analysis_data,
  aoi_col = "aoi_current",
  target_aoi_values = "target",
  subject_col = "subject",
  condition_col = "condition",
  time_col = "time",
  trial_col = "trial_global",
  time_window = c(0, 10000),
  bin_size_ms = 250,
  denominator = "valid",
  min_denominator_samples = 3
))

aoi_gamm <- if (!is_error(aoi_gamm_data)) {
  safe_eval(fit_gazepoint_aoi_gamm(
    aoi_gamm_data,
    time_k = 8,
    min_rows = 10,
    min_subjects = 2,
    min_time_bins = 4
  ))
} else aoi_gamm_data

aoi_gamm_plot <- if (!is_error(aoi_gamm)) safe_eval(plot_gazepoint_aoi_gamm(aoi_gamm)) else aoi_gamm

bind_rows(
  object_summary(aoi_gamm_data, "prepare_gazepoint_aoi_gamm_data"),
  object_summary(aoi_gamm, "fit_gazepoint_aoi_gamm"),
  object_summary(aoi_gamm_plot, "plot_gazepoint_aoi_gamm")
) %>%
  kable()
```

```{r aoi-gamm-plot, fig.cap="AOI target-looking GAMM trajectory by condition, when the model is available."}
if (!is_error(aoi_gamm_plot)) print(aoi_gamm_plot)
```

# 11. Fixation and event-aligned workflow

```{r fixation-workflow}
fixation_trial_summary <- safe_eval(summarise_gazepoint_fixation_trials(
  fixation_data,
  group_cols = c("subject", "media_id", "trial_global", "condition"),
  start_col = "time",
  duration_col = "duration",
  x_col = "x",
  y_col = "y",
  valid_col = "valid",
  aoi_col = "aoi_current",
  target_aoi_values = "target"
))

aligned_data <- safe_eval(prepare_gazepoint_fixation_aligned_data(
  analysis_data,
  time_col = "time",
  participant_col = "subject",
  trial_col = "trial_global",
  aoi_col = "aoi_current",
  target_aoi = "target",
  fixation_col = "is_fixation",
  saccade_col = "is_saccade",
  alignment_event = "first_target_entry",
  baseline_window = c(-500, 0),
  analysis_window = c(0, 2000),
  keep_unaligned = TRUE
))

bind_rows(
  object_summary(fixation_data, "fixation_data"),
  object_summary(fixation_trial_summary, "summarise_gazepoint_fixation_trials"),
  object_summary(aligned_data, "prepare_gazepoint_fixation_aligned_data")
) %>%
  kable()
```

```{r fixation-summary-plot, fig.cap="Fixation count per trial by condition."}
fixation_count_plot_data <- if (!is_error(fixation_data) && is.data.frame(fixation_data)) {
  fixation_data %>%
    group_by(subject, condition, trial_global) %>%
    summarise(
      fixation_count = n(),
      mean_fixation_duration_ms = mean(duration, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
} else {
  tibble()
}

if (nrow(fixation_count_plot_data) > 0) {
  fixation_count_plot_data %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = condition, y = fixation_count)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    labs(x = "Condition", y = "Fixation count per trial") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))
}
```

# 12. Transition, scanpath, and sequence workflow

```{r transition-workflow}
aoi_entries <- safe_eval(summarise_gazepoint_aoi_entries(
  analysis_data,
  aoi_col = "aoi_current",
  time_col = "time",
  group_cols = c("subject", "media_id", "trial_global")
))

aoi_transitions <- safe_eval(summarise_gazepoint_aoi_transitions(
  analysis_data,
  aoi_col = "aoi_current",
  time_col = "time",
  group_cols = c("subject", "media_id", "trial_global")
))

aoi_trial_features <- safe_eval(summarise_gazepoint_aoi_trial_features(
  analysis_data,
  aoi_col = "aoi_current",
  time_col = "time",
  group_cols = c("subject", "media_id", "trial_global", "condition"),
  target_aoi_values = "target"
))

aoi_sequences <- safe_eval(prepare_gazepoint_aoi_sequences(
  analysis_data,
  aoi_col = "aoi_current",
  time_col = "time",
  group_cols = c("subject", "media_id", "trial_global")
))

transition_matrix <- safe_eval(compute_gazepoint_aoi_transition_matrix(
  analysis_data,
  aoi_col = "aoi_current",
  time_col = "time",
  group_cols = c("subject", "media_id", "trial_global"),
  by_cols = "condition"
))

markov_object <- safe_eval(create_gazepoint_markovchain_object(
  analysis_data,
  state_col = "aoi_current",
  participant_col = "subject",
  trial_col = "trial_global",
  time_col = "time"
))

hmm_data <- safe_eval(prepare_gazepoint_hmm_data(
  analysis_data,
  state_col = "aoi_current",
  participant_col = "subject",
  trial_col = "trial_global",
  time_col = "time",
  observation_cols = c("x", "y", "pupil"),
  covariate_cols = "condition"
))

semimarkov_data <- safe_eval(prepare_gazepoint_semimarkov_data(
  analysis_data,
  state_col = "aoi_current",
  participant_col = "subject",
  trial_col = "trial_global",
  time_col = "time",
  covariate_cols = "condition"
))

bind_rows(
  object_summary(aoi_entries, "summarise_gazepoint_aoi_entries"),
  object_summary(aoi_transitions, "summarise_gazepoint_aoi_transitions"),
  object_summary(aoi_trial_features, "summarise_gazepoint_aoi_trial_features"),
  object_summary(aoi_sequences, "prepare_gazepoint_aoi_sequences"),
  object_summary(transition_matrix, "compute_gazepoint_aoi_transition_matrix"),
  object_summary(markov_object, "create_gazepoint_markovchain_object"),
  object_summary(hmm_data, "prepare_gazepoint_hmm_data"),
  object_summary(semimarkov_data, "prepare_gazepoint_semimarkov_data")
) %>%
  kable()
```

```{r transition-plot, fig.cap="AOI transition matrix plot."}
transition_plot <- if (!is_error(transition_matrix)) safe_eval(plot_gazepoint_aoi_transition_matrix(transition_matrix)) else transition_matrix
if (!is_error(transition_plot)) print(transition_plot)
```

```{r time-varying-transition-and-nb}
transition_events <- analysis_data %>%
  arrange(subject, media_id, trial_global, time) %>%
  group_by(subject, media_id, trial_global, condition) %>%
  mutate(from_aoi = aoi_current, to_aoi = lead(aoi_current), transition_time = time) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(!is.na(from_aoi), !is.na(to_aoi), from_aoi != to_aoi)

time_varying_transition <- safe_eval(compute_gazepoint_time_varying_transition_matrix(
  transition_events,
  from_col = "from_aoi",
  to_col = "to_aoi",
  time_col = "transition_time",
  window_size_ms = 1000,
  by_cols = "condition",
  normalise = "row"
))

transition_count_data <- transition_events %>%
  count(subject, condition, from_aoi, to_aoi, name = "transition_count") %>%
  mutate(exposure = 1)

transition_nb_sensitivity <- safe_eval(fit_gazepoint_transition_count_nb_sensitivity(
  transition_count_data,
  count_col = "transition_count",
  from_col = "from_aoi",
  to_col = "to_aoi",
  condition_cols = "condition",
  random_effect_cols = "subject",
  exposure_col = "exposure",
  family = "nbinom2",
  zero_inflation = FALSE
))

bind_rows(
  object_summary(transition_events, "derived_transition_events"),
  object_summary(time_varying_transition, "compute_gazepoint_time_varying_transition_matrix"),
  object_summary(transition_count_data, "derived_transition_count_data"),
  object_summary(transition_nb_sensitivity, "fit_gazepoint_transition_count_nb_sensitivity")
) %>%
  kable()
```

# 13. Model checking, prediction, leave-one-out, and divergence-point sensitivity

```{r model-support}
aoi_model_table <- workflow_results$aoi_table %>%
  mutate(
    condition = media_design$condition[match(MEDIA_ID, media_design$media_id)],
    subject = paste0("paper_user_", sprintf("%02d", USER_ID))
  )

lm_small <- lm(sample_time_viewed_sec ~ condition, data = aoi_model_table)
lm_large <- lm(sample_time_viewed_sec ~ condition + fixation_count, data = aoi_model_table)

nested_comparison <- safe_eval(compare_gazepoint_nested_models(
  models = list(lm_small, lm_large),
  model_names = c("condition_only", "condition_plus_fixations")
))

model_summary <- safe_eval(tidy_gazepoint_model_summary(
  lm_large,
  model_name = "AOI viewed-time linear model"
))

loo_result <- safe_eval(run_gazepoint_model_leave_one_out(
  data = aoi_model_table,
  unit_col = "subject",
  fit_function = function(d) lm(sample_time_viewed_sec ~ condition + fixation_count, data = d),
  extract_function = function(model) {
    data.frame(
      term = names(stats::coef(model)),
      estimate = unname(stats::coef(model)),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }
))

prediction_plot <- safe_eval(plot_gazepoint_model_predictions(
  data = aoi_model_table,
  model = lm_large,
  x_col = "fixation_count",
  outcome_col = "sample_time_viewed_sec",
  condition_col = "condition"
))

aoi_glmm_model_object <- if (!is_error(aoi_glmm) && is.list(aoi_glmm) && "model" %in% names(aoi_glmm)) aoi_glmm$model else NULL

aoi_model_convergence <- if (!is.null(aoi_glmm_model_object)) safe_eval(check_gazepoint_model_convergence(aoi_glmm_model_object, model_name = "AOI window GLMM")) else safe_eval(stop("AOI model object unavailable."))
aoi_model_singularity <- if (!is.null(aoi_glmm_model_object)) safe_eval(check_gazepoint_model_singularity(aoi_glmm_model_object, model_name = "AOI window GLMM")) else safe_eval(stop("AOI model object unavailable."))
aoi_model_overdispersion <- if (!is.null(aoi_glmm_model_object)) safe_eval(check_gazepoint_model_overdispersion(aoi_glmm_model_object, model_name = "AOI window GLMM")) else safe_eval(stop("AOI model object unavailable."))

model_export_dir <- file.path(paper_tables_dir, "model_tables")
dir.create(model_export_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
exported_model_tables <- safe_eval(export_gazepoint_model_tables(
  model_summary = model_summary,
  output_dir = model_export_dir,
  prefix = "paper_showcase_model",
  overwrite = TRUE
))

bind_rows(
  object_summary(nested_comparison, "compare_gazepoint_nested_models"),
  object_summary(model_summary, "tidy_gazepoint_model_summary"),
  object_summary(loo_result, "run_gazepoint_model_leave_one_out"),
  object_summary(prediction_plot, "plot_gazepoint_model_predictions"),
  object_summary(aoi_model_convergence, "check_gazepoint_model_convergence"),
  object_summary(aoi_model_singularity, "check_gazepoint_model_singularity"),
  object_summary(aoi_model_overdispersion, "check_gazepoint_model_overdispersion"),
  object_summary(exported_model_tables, "export_gazepoint_model_tables")
) %>%
  kable()
```

```{r prediction-plot, fig.cap="Observed and model-implied AOI viewed-time predictions."}
if (!is_error(prediction_plot)) print(prediction_plot)
```

```{r divergence-point}
pupil_timecourse_for_divergence <- analysis_data %>%
  group_by(subject, trial_global, condition, time_bin = floor(time / 250) * 250) %>%
  summarise(mean_pupil = mean(pupil, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(mean_pupil = if_else(is.finite(mean_pupil), mean_pupil, NA_real_)) %>%
  rename(time = time_bin) %>%
  filter(condition %in% c("early_target", "late_target"))

divergence_point <- safe_eval(estimate_gazepoint_divergence_point(
  pupil_timecourse_for_divergence,
  outcome_col = "mean_pupil",
  time_col = "time",
  condition_col = "condition",
  participant_col = "subject",
  trial_col = "trial_global",
  comparison = c("late_target", "early_target"),
  n_boot = 100,
  consecutive_points = 2,
  seed = 20260617,
  keep_bootstrap = FALSE
))

object_summary(divergence_point, "estimate_gazepoint_divergence_point") %>%
  kable()
```

# 14. Multiverse and cluster-preparation utilities

```{r multiverse-cluster}
preprocessing_registry <- safe_eval(create_gazepoint_preprocessing_registry())
preprocessing_multiverse <- safe_eval(create_gazepoint_preprocessing_multiverse())

cluster_data <- safe_eval(prepare_gazepoint_cluster_data(
  pupil_timecourse_for_divergence,
  outcome_col = "mean_pupil",
  subject_col = "subject",
  condition_col = "condition",
  time_col = "time",
  trial_col = "trial_global"
))

small_pupil_multiverse <- if (!is_error(preprocessing_multiverse) && is.list(preprocessing_multiverse)) {
  safe_eval(run_gazepoint_pupil_multiverse(
    analysis_data,
    multiverse = preprocessing_multiverse,
    branch_ids = NULL,
    pupil_col = "pupil",
    time_col = "time",
    group_cols = c("subject", "media_id", "condition", "trial_global"),
    summarise_windows = TRUE,
    windows = c(0, 1000, 3000),
    keep_outputs = FALSE,
    stop_on_error = FALSE
  ))
} else preprocessing_multiverse

small_aoi_multiverse <- if (!is_error(preprocessing_multiverse) && is.list(preprocessing_multiverse)) {
  safe_eval(run_gazepoint_aoi_multiverse(
    analysis_data,
    multiverse = preprocessing_multiverse,
    windows = aoi_windows_def,
    time_col = "time",
    aoi_col = "aoi_current",
    subject_col = "subject",
    condition_col = "condition",
    group_cols = c("subject", "media_id", "trial_global"),
    target_aoi_values = "target",
    keep_outputs = FALSE,
    stop_on_error = FALSE
  ))
} else preprocessing_multiverse

bind_rows(
  object_summary(preprocessing_registry, "create_gazepoint_preprocessing_registry"),
  object_summary(preprocessing_multiverse, "create_gazepoint_preprocessing_multiverse"),
  object_summary(cluster_data, "prepare_gazepoint_cluster_data"),
  object_summary(small_pupil_multiverse, "run_gazepoint_pupil_multiverse"),
  object_summary(small_aoi_multiverse, "run_gazepoint_aoi_multiverse")
) %>%
  kable()
```

# 15. Reporting checklist, readiness gate, exclusions, and decision audit

```{r reporting-readiness-exclusions}
readiness_gate <- safe_eval(check_gazepoint_real_data_readiness(
  analysis_data,
  analysis_type = "combined",
  participant_col = "subject",
  trial_col = "trial_global",
  time_col = "time",
  condition_col = "condition",
  stimulus_col = "media_id",
  aoi_col = "aoi_current",
  pupil_col = "pupil",
  gaze_x_col = "x",
  gaze_y_col = "y",
  tracking_valid_col = "valid",
  min_rows = 100,
  min_participants = 2,
  min_trials = 2
))

exclusion_recommendations <- safe_eval(recommend_gazepoint_exclusions(
  analysis_data,
  participant_col = "subject",
  trial_col = "trial_global",
  condition_col = "condition",
  validity_col = "valid",
  x_col = "x",
  y_col = "y",
  pupil_col = "pupil",
  min_trial_samples = 10
))

reporting_checklist <- safe_eval(create_gazepoint_reporting_checklist(
  data = analysis_data,
  objects = list(
    workflow_summary = workflow_summary,
    flagged_quality = workflow_results$flagged_quality,
    aoi_table = workflow_results$aoi_table,
    readiness_gate = readiness_gate,
    pupil_lmm = pupil_lmm,
    aoi_glmm = aoi_glmm
  ),
  analysis_type = "combined",
  study_title = "Paper-only synthetic Gazepoint workflow showcase"
))

design_balance <- safe_eval(audit_gazepoint_design_balance(
  analysis_data,
  subject_col = "subject",
  condition_col = "condition",
  unit_cols = c("media_id", "trial_global")
))

condition_quality_imbalance <- safe_eval(audit_gazepoint_condition_quality_imbalance(
  workflow_results$flagged_quality %>%
    mutate(condition = media_design$condition[match(MEDIA_ID, media_design$media_id)]),
  condition_col = "condition",
  quality_cols = c("BPOGV_valid_pct", "min_pupil_valid_pct_observed"),
  subject_col = "USER_FILE"
))

analysis_with_exclusion_status <- analysis_data %>%
  mutate(
    exclusion_status = if_else(!valid | is.na(pupil), "review", "ok"),
    exclusion_reason = case_when(
      !valid ~ "invalid_gaze_sample",
      is.na(pupil) ~ "missing_pupil",
      TRUE ~ "retained"
    ),
    excluded_trial = exclusion_status == "review"
  )

exclusion_flow <- safe_eval(audit_gazepoint_exclusion_flow(
  analysis_with_exclusion_status,
  subject_col = "subject",
  condition_col = "condition",
  unit_cols = c("media_id", "trial_global"),
  status_col = "exclusion_status",
  reason_col = "exclusion_reason",
  min_retained_prop = 0.7
))

post_exclusion_balance <- safe_eval(audit_gazepoint_post_exclusion_balance(
  analysis_with_exclusion_status,
  subject_col = "subject",
  condition_col = "condition",
  unit_cols = c("media_id", "trial_global"),
  status_col = "exclusion_status",
  min_retained_units_per_condition = 1L,
  min_retained_units_per_subject_condition = 1L,
  require_all_conditions_per_subject = FALSE
))

analysis_decision_audit <- safe_eval(create_gazepoint_analysis_decision_audit(
  results = list(
    readiness_gate = readiness_gate,
    exclusion_recommendations = exclusion_recommendations,
    exclusion_flow = exclusion_flow,
    post_exclusion_balance = post_exclusion_balance,
    aoi_denominator_audit = aoi_denominator_audit,
    pupil_reliability = pupil_reliability
  ),
  required_confirmatory = c("readiness_gate", "exclusion_recommendations", "aoi_denominator_audit"),
  diagnostics_required = TRUE,
  require_clean_diagnostics = FALSE
))

bind_rows(
  object_summary(readiness_gate, "check_gazepoint_real_data_readiness"),
  object_summary(exclusion_recommendations, "recommend_gazepoint_exclusions"),
  object_summary(reporting_checklist, "create_gazepoint_reporting_checklist"),
  object_summary(design_balance, "audit_gazepoint_design_balance"),
  object_summary(condition_quality_imbalance, "audit_gazepoint_condition_quality_imbalance"),
  object_summary(exclusion_flow, "audit_gazepoint_exclusion_flow"),
  object_summary(post_exclusion_balance, "audit_gazepoint_post_exclusion_balance"),
  object_summary(analysis_decision_audit, "create_gazepoint_analysis_decision_audit")
) %>%
  kable()
```

```{r exclusion-tables}
show_component(exclusion_recommendations, "participant_recommendations", n = 12)
show_component(exclusion_recommendations, "trial_recommendations", n = 12)
```

# 16. Interoperability and optional external-package checks

```{r interoperability}
eyetools_data <- safe_eval(prepare_gazepoint_eyetools_data(
  analysis_data,
  participant_col = "subject",
  trial_col = "trial_global",
  time_col = "time",
  x_col = "x",
  y_col = "y",
  pupil_col = "pupil",
  media_col = "media_id",
  condition_col = "condition",
  aoi_col = "aoi_current",
  fixation_col = "is_fixation",
  validity_cols = "valid"
))

gazer_data <- safe_eval(prepare_gazepoint_gazer_data(
  analysis_data,
  participant_col = "subject",
  trial_col = "trial_global",
  time_col = "time",
  x_col = "x",
  y_col = "y",
  pupil_col = "pupil",
  media_col = "media_id",
  condition_col = "condition",
  aoi_col = "aoi_current",
  fixation_col = "is_fixation",
  validity_cols = "valid"
))

pupillometryr_data <- safe_eval(prepare_gazepoint_pupillometryr_data(
  analysis_data,
  participant_col = "subject",
  trial_col = "trial_global",
  time_col = "time",
  pupil_col = "pupil",
  media_col = "media_id",
  condition_col = "condition",
  event_col = NULL,
  validity_cols = "valid"
))

eyetrackingr_data <- safe_eval(prepare_gazepoint_eyetrackingr_data(
  analysis_data,
  participant_col = "subject",
  trial_col = "trial_global",
  time_col = "time",
  x_col = "x",
  y_col = "y",
  media_col = "media_id",
  condition_col = "condition",
  aoi_col = "aoi_current",
  validity_cols = "valid"
))

gazer_crosscheck <- safe_eval(run_gazepoint_gazer_crosscheck(
  analysis_data,
  participant_col = "subject",
  trial_col = "trial_global",
  time_col = "time",
  pupil_col = "pupil",
  condition_col = "condition",
  hz = 60
))

eyetools_detection <- safe_eval(run_gazepoint_eyetools_fixation_detection(
  analysis_data,
  participant_col = "subject",
  trial_col = "trial_global",
  time_col = "time",
  x_col = "x",
  y_col = "y",
  condition_col = "condition",
  stimulus_col = "media_id",
  method = "dispersion",
  sample_rate = 60
))

bind_rows(
  object_summary(eyetools_data, "prepare_gazepoint_eyetools_data"),
  object_summary(gazer_data, "prepare_gazepoint_gazer_data"),
  object_summary(pupillometryr_data, "prepare_gazepoint_pupillometryr_data"),
  object_summary(eyetrackingr_data, "prepare_gazepoint_eyetrackingr_data"),
  object_summary(gazer_crosscheck, "run_gazepoint_gazer_crosscheck"),
  object_summary(eyetools_detection, "run_gazepoint_eyetools_fixation_detection")
) %>%
  kable()
```

# 17. Complete software feature inventory

This inventory is a reproducible check of the exported `gp3tools` surface. The paper text should not claim that every function is statistically necessary for every study. Instead, it should describe the feature families and show that the richer synthetic dataset exercises the major workflow classes.

```{r function-inventory}
exports <- sort(getNamespaceExports("gp3tools"))

function_inventory <- tibble(function_name = exports) %>%
  mutate(
    feature_area = case_when(
      grepl("workflow|report|checklist|readiness|quality|sampling", function_name) ~ "Workflow, diagnostics, and reporting",
      grepl("pupil|blink|hampel|baseline|interpolat|reliability", function_name) ~ "Pupil preprocessing and pupil analysis",
      grepl("aoi|fixation|saccade", function_name) ~ "AOI, fixation, and saccade analysis",
      grepl("transition|scanpath|markov|hmm|semimarkov|sequence", function_name) ~ "Transitions, scanpaths, and sequence preparation",
      grepl("model|glm|lmm|gamm|prediction|nested|loo|sensitivity|divergence", function_name) ~ "Modeling, time-course analysis, and sensitivity checks",
      grepl("prepare|export|adapter|eyetools|gazer|eyetrackingr|pupillometryr", function_name) ~ "Adapters and interoperability",
      grepl("plot|visual", function_name) ~ "Visualization",
      TRUE ~ "General utilities"
    )
  )

function_inventory %>%
  count(feature_area, name = "n_functions") %>%
  arrange(desc(n_functions)) %>%
  kable()

function_inventory %>%
  arrange(feature_area, function_name) %>%
  kable()
```



# 18. Software demonstration results

The paper-only synthetic export folder was successfully processed by the high-level `gp3tools` workflow. The workflow identified 48 complete all-gaze/fixation file pairs and no problematic file pairs. The resulting analysis objects contained 115,200 all-gaze rows, 5,691 fixation rows, 192 sampling-rate rows, 192 tracking-quality rows, 1,344 AOI summary rows, and 60 recording-level rows requiring review. The workflow also produced standard CSV outputs, diagnostic plots, and an HTML report. These results show that the package can process a moderately sized Gazepoint-like export folder and return a coherent set of diagnostic, summary, plotting, and reporting objects.

```{r manuscript-results-workflow-summary}
workflow_summary %>%
  transmute(
    `All-gaze rows` = all_gaze_rows,
    `Fixation rows` = fixation_rows,
    `Sampling rows` = sampling_rows,
    `Tracking-quality rows` = tracking_quality_rows,
    `AOI rows` = aoi_rows,
    `Review-required rows` = review_required_rows,
    `Complete file pairs` = complete_file_pairs,
    `Problem file pairs` = problem_file_pairs,
    `Output table files` = output_table_files,
    `Output plot files` = output_plot_files,
    `Report created` = report_created
  ) %>%
  knitr::kable()
```

The sampling-rate diagnostics confirmed that all synthetic recordings were sampled at the nominal 60 Hz rate. This is expected because the paper-only generator created regularly spaced synthetic samples. The sampling-rate result is therefore best interpreted as a positive workflow check rather than as evidence of sampling variability. The compact manuscript plot summarises the same information without displaying every recording label.

```{r manuscript-results-sampling-summary}
workflow_results$sampling %>%
  mutate(condition = media_design$condition[match(MEDIA_ID, media_design$media_id)]) %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  summarise(
    n_recordings = n(),
    mean_hz = mean(estimated_hz, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_hz = min(estimated_hz, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_hz = max(estimated_hz, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_interval_ms = mean(mean_interval_ms, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  knitr::kable(digits = 3)
```

The tracking-quality diagnostics were more informative. The generator intentionally introduced invalid gaze samples, out-of-range coordinates, missing pupil intervals, and participant-level quality variation. The resulting quality-control table identified review-required recordings, especially in the distractor-heavy condition. This demonstrates that `gp3tools` can flag recordings requiring inspection while preserving the full diagnostic table for transparent reporting.

```{r manuscript-results-quality-summary}
workflow_results$flagged_quality %>%
  mutate(
    condition = media_design$condition[match(MEDIA_ID, media_design$media_id)],
    review_required_clean = tidyr::replace_na(review_required, FALSE)
  ) %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  summarise(
    n_recordings = n(),
    review_required = sum(review_required_clean),
    review_required_percent = 100 * mean(review_required_clean),
    mean_gaze_valid_pct = mean(BPOGV_valid_pct, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_gaze_valid_pct = min(BPOGV_valid_pct, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_gaze_valid_pct = max(BPOGV_valid_pct, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  knitr::kable(digits = 2)
```

The AOI workflow generated interpretable condition-specific summaries. The early-target and late-target conditions showed higher target-viewing summaries around their intended temporal regions, while the distractor-heavy condition showed increased distractor viewing. These synthetic patterns are useful because they exercise AOI-window summaries, AOI denominator auditing, AOI GLMM preparation, empirical-logit sensitivity, AOI time-course GAMM fitting, and transition-matrix construction. They should not be interpreted as substantive perceptual effects.

```{r manuscript-results-aoi-summary}
workflow_results$aoi_table %>%
  mutate(condition = media_design$condition[match(MEDIA_ID, media_design$media_id)]) %>%
  group_by(condition, AOI) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_viewed_sec = mean(sample_time_viewed_sec, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_fixation_count = mean(fixation_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_ttff_sec = mean(sample_ttff_sec, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(condition, desc(mean_viewed_sec)) %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  slice_head(n = 4) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  knitr::kable(digits = 3)
```

The pupil workflow also produced a useful software demonstration. Pupil preprocessing functions completed successfully, pupil-window summaries were created for raw, linear-interpolated, and PCHIP branches, baseline and gap audits completed, confirmatory pupil-window model data were prepared, and representative LMM, sensitivity, GAMM, and PFE-GAMM objects were created. The synthetic pupil time course shows clear condition-specific trajectories, which makes it suitable as a main demonstration figure. The interpolation-sensitivity results should be described cautiously: the linear interpolation branch produced small differences from raw window means, while the PCHIP branch produced negligible differences in this synthetic run.

```{r manuscript-results-pupil-summary}
bind_rows(
  object_summary(pupil_windows_raw, "Raw pupil windows"),
  object_summary(pupil_windows_linear, "Linear-interpolated pupil windows"),
  object_summary(pupil_windows_pchip, "PCHIP-interpolated pupil windows"),
  object_summary(pupil_model_data, "Pupil-window model data"),
  object_summary(pupil_lmm, "Pupil-window LMM"),
  object_summary(pupil_sensitivity, "Pupil-window sensitivity"),
  object_summary(pupil_gamm, "Pupil GAMM"),
  object_summary(pupil_pfe_gamm, "PFE-GAMM sensitivity")
) %>%
  select(object, status, class, rows, columns) %>%
  knitr::kable()
```

The fixation and transition workflows benefited from the richer synthetic fixation files. The final paper-only dataset contains 5,691 fixation rows, corresponding to approximately 29.6 fixations per participant-by-media recording. This made it possible to demonstrate fixation-trial summaries, fixation-aligned objects, AOI entries, AOI transitions, transition matrices, time-varying transition matrices, Markov-style objects, HMM-ready data, semi-Markov-ready data, and negative-binomial transition-count sensitivity.

```{r manuscript-results-fixation-transition-summary}
fixation_count_plot_data %>%
  summarise(
    n_trials = n(),
    mean_fixation_count = mean(fixation_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_fixation_count = min(fixation_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_fixation_count = max(fixation_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_fixation_duration_ms = mean(mean_fixation_duration_ms, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  knitr::kable(digits = 2)

bind_rows(
  object_summary(fixation_trial_summary, "Fixation-trial summary"),
  object_summary(aligned_data, "Fixation-aligned data"),
  object_summary(aoi_entries, "AOI entries"),
  object_summary(aoi_transitions, "AOI transitions"),
  object_summary(transition_matrix, "AOI transition matrix"),
  object_summary(time_varying_transition, "Time-varying transition matrix"),
  object_summary(markov_object, "Markov-style object"),
  object_summary(hmm_data, "HMM-ready data"),
  object_summary(semimarkov_data, "Semi-Markov-ready data"),
  object_summary(transition_nb_sensitivity, "NB transition-count sensitivity")
) %>%
  select(object, status, class, rows, columns) %>%
  knitr::kable()
```

Finally, the reporting and interoperability workflows completed successfully. The RMD generated readiness-gate objects, exclusion recommendations, a reporting checklist, design-balance and condition-quality audits, exclusion-flow audits, post-exclusion balance checks, a final analysis-decision audit, and adapter outputs for `eyetools`, `gazer`, `pupillometryR`-style, and `eyetrackingR`-style workflows. This supports the central software-paper claim that `gp3tools` is not only an importer, but a complete workflow scaffold for transparent Gazepoint analysis in R.

```{r manuscript-results-reporting-interoperability-summary}
bind_rows(
  object_summary(readiness_gate, "Real-data readiness gate"),
  object_summary(exclusion_recommendations, "Exclusion recommendations"),
  object_summary(reporting_checklist, "Reporting checklist"),
  object_summary(analysis_decision_audit, "Analysis-decision audit"),
  object_summary(eyetools_data, "eyetools adapter data"),
  object_summary(gazer_data, "gazer adapter data"),
  object_summary(pupillometryr_data, "pupillometryR-style adapter data"),
  object_summary(eyetrackingr_data, "eyetrackingR-style adapter data"),
  object_summary(gazer_crosscheck, "gazeR cross-check"),
  object_summary(eyetools_detection, "eyetools fixation detection")
) %>%
  select(object, status, class, rows, columns) %>%
  knitr::kable()
```

Overall, the synthetic demonstration confirms that the main package workflow executes end to end on a realistic Gazepoint-like folder, while the detailed module summaries show that the package covers import, quality control, pupil preprocessing, AOI modelling, fixation summaries, transition analysis, model diagnostics, reporting, and interoperability. Because the data are synthetic, the numerical condition differences should be described only as designed demonstration patterns.




# 19. Recommended manuscript figures and tables

The computational sections above are intentionally comprehensive because this R Markdown file is designed to exercise the package workflow. A journal article should not include every diagnostic object, full table, or complete function inventory in the main text. The recommended manuscript strategy is to use a small number of high-information figures and tables in the main article and move detailed diagnostics, object summaries, complete function inventories, and session information to supplementary material.

The strongest main-text figures are the workflow architecture or function-family overview, the pupil time course by condition, the AOI target-looking GAMM trajectory, the AOI viewed-time summary by condition and AOI, the before/after drift-correction plot, and either the AOI transition probability heatmap or the model-prediction plot. The strongest main-text tables are the dataset/workflow summary, the file-pair and quality-control summary, the object-status summary for major workflow modules, and a compact table mapping package tasks to key helper functions.

The automated sampling-rate and tracking-quality report plots remain useful as diagnostic report outputs, but they are visually dense when all 192 recording-level rows are shown. For manuscript use, the polished compact versions above summarise the same diagnostic information by condition and quality status without displaying every recording name on the y-axis.

```{r recommended-manuscript-figures}
recommended_figures <- tibble::tribble(
  ~figure, ~source_section_or_chunk, ~recommended_use, ~notes,
  "Figure 1", "Workflow architecture / function families", "Main text", "Create as a conceptual diagram from the function-family inventory rather than as a raw code output.",
  "Figure 2", "pupil-timecourse", "Main text", "Best visual demonstration of time-course pupil workflows and condition-specific synthetic trajectories.",
  "Figure 3", "aoi-gamm-plot", "Main text", "Best visual demonstration of AOI time-course modelling and target-looking trajectories.",
  "Figure 4", "aoi-viewed-plot", "Main text or supplement", "Useful compact summary of AOI viewed time by condition and AOI.",
  "Figure 5", "drift-plot", "Main text or supplement", "Use after relabelling stages as Before and After; demonstrates optional gaze recalibration.",
  "Figure 6", "transition-plot", "Supplement or main text if space allows", "Useful for scanpath/transition feature demonstration but visually dense.",
  "Figure S1", "sampling-plot", "Supplement", "Compact sampling-rate diagnostic figure saved to the paper figures folder.",
  "Figure S2", "quality-plot", "Supplement", "Compact tracking-quality diagnostic figure saved to the paper figures folder.",
  "Figure S3", "prediction-plot", "Supplement", "Illustrates model-implied prediction plotting rather than a substantive empirical effect."
)

recommended_figures %>%
  knitr::kable()
```

```{r recommended-manuscript-tables}
recommended_tables <- tibble::tribble(
  ~table, ~source_section_or_chunk, ~recommended_use, ~notes,
  "Table 1", "workflow-execution", "Main text", "Dataset and workflow summary: rows, file pairs, AOI rows, fixation rows, and report creation.",
  "Table 2", "quality-diagnostics", "Main text or supplement", "Recording-level review summary by condition; report NA review values as not flagged where appropriate.",
```
